title: Abutilon avicennae
taxonomy: Abutilon avicennae belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow family. This family includes over 200 species of flowering plants, including okra, hibiscus, and cotton. The genus Abutilon contains around 100 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to the Americas. Abutilon avicennae is a species of flowering plant that is native to the Middle East and North Africa. It is a member of the Malvaceae family and is closely related to other species within this family.

intro: Abutilon avicennae, also known as Indian mallow, is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Abutilon avicennae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
